name: code-from-kb
description: Turn a Rust coding request into compiler-verified code. Routes to the relevant KB entry for grounding, generates code against that context, then verifies and repairs against cargo. An authored, stateless chain: each node declares exactly the inputs it sees.

nodes.comment: Constrained pick of the KB entry whose idioms are relevant. Model proposes from an enum; code executes.
CODE opens the chosen entries (no skippable tool) and assembles reference context. The model is not in this node.
Constrained {code} generation. Sees the request and the KB context for idioms — NOT entry_ids, NOT the routing rationale.
The deterministic oracle: cargo clippy -D warnings + a real run, with bounded repair. Sees only request + code.
